management_notes: Exceptional by junior mining standards. CEO Dr. Diane Nicolson is a genuine economic geologist (20+ years international base and precious metals experience, former Head of Business Development at Hunter Dickinson Services Inc., former chair of AME BC) — not a capital markets promoter. Chairman Robert Dickinson (Canadian Mining Hall of Fame inductee, co-founder Hunter Dickinson Inc.) holds approximately 17–19% of shares through Sutton Group, providing deep skin-in-the-game alignment. HDI's pedigree includes Taseko Mines, Northern Dynasty Minerals, and multiple copper discoveries built and monetised in BC. The decision by Freeport-McMoRan to commit $75M in Stage 2 JV funding — and separately by Boliden to fund $30M at DUKE — is the strongest possible third-party validation of management's geological and commercial capabilities. New VP Exploration Gavin Titley P.Geo and Chief Exploration Scientist Dr. Farhad Bouzari (both appointed February 2026) strengthen the technical bench. The 23.7-year average board tenure signals stability. One modest deduction: Hudbay exited the IKE earn-in in January 2019, though the JOY/DUKE JV wins are strong countervailing evidence.
geology_notes: AuRORA deposit (JOY District, Toodoggone-Kemess belt, north-central BC): high-grade Au-Cu-Ag porphyry with world-class intercepts — 231 m @ 0.83 g/t Au + 0.22% Cu; 126 m @ 0.97 g/t Au + 0.32% Cu; 300 m @ 0.51 g/t Au + 0.23% Cu from TWINS (Feb 2026 discovery). Deposit area at least 1.4 km x 800 m, open in multiple directions. 35 holes (15,381 m) drilled at JOY in 2025 alone. The Toodoggone-Kemess belt hosts Red Chris and Kemess South/North — proven world-class porphyry terrain. Freeport's $75M Stage 2 commitment is the most credible external geological validation available. TWINS and CANYON are new February 2026 discoveries, suggesting a multi-deposit district. IKE District (south-central BC): large bulk-tonnage Cu-Mo-Ag porphyry system (IKE main, 2020 NI 43-101 on file), with higher-grade Empress Cu-Au sub-zone under active drilling. DUKE District (central BC): advancing porphyry Cu-Au under 60:40 JV with Boliden. Three separate district-scale porphyry systems in Tier 1 BC jurisdiction. Score 4 rather than 5: no published NI 43-101 resource on AuRORA (the primary value driver) yet, and IKE main deposit grade is likely sub-economic on a standalone basis.
capital_notes: Share structure is relatively clean at ~225M shares outstanding for a company with 10+ years of exploration history across three district-scale BC porphyry projects — a credit to the JV model. JV structure with Freeport (up to C$110M non-dilutive via JOY) and Boliden (C$30M completed at DUKE) allows Amarc to run multi-district exploration programs without issuing shares for drilling costs. Warrants from the December 2023 financing are modest (approximately 4.8M at C$0.08, near-expired). Fully diluted share count not confirmed from filed financials. Key negative: corporate working capital is thin — approximately C$1.56M cash with a C$1M shareholder loan (extended November 2024 to November 2025) that signals limited overhead buffer. Boliden declining Stage 2 at DUKE (removing C$60M of potential future non-dilutive funding) increases the likelihood of future self-funded dilution at that project. Score 3: good structure with meaningful near-term working capital risk.
catalyst_notes: Among the richest near-term catalyst pipelines in Canadian junior copper exploration: (1) 2026 summer drill season at JOY imminently launching — Freeport is contractually obligated to spend minimum C$10M/year under Stage 2 terms, so this program will happen; (2) Approximately 12 remaining 2025 AuRORA expansion hole assays not yet released as of Q4 2025; (3) TWINS and CANYON follow-up drilling in 2026 season; (4) AuRORA NI 43-101 resource estimate — no firm date announced, but drilling data density is building toward it and it is the single most important potential catalyst for the stock; (5) Frankfurt Stock Exchange listing (AQ5) expands investor access; (6) Company named to 2026 TSX Venture 50 (21st place by share performance). The pending AuRORA resource estimate is a transformational catalyst — the first formal quantification of the discovery that attracted Freeport's $75M+ commitment.
acquisition_notes: Market cap approximately C$209M at C$0.93/share on ~225M shares. No NI 43-101 resource has been published on AuRORA, making a rigorous NAV calculation impossible. JV implied valuation heuristic: Freeport paid C$35M for 60% at Stage 1 (implying C$58M total project value at Stage 1), then committed C$75M for an additional 10% at Stage 2 (implying C$750M+ project value on a per-percentage basis), suggesting Amarc's 40% stake in Aurora Minerals (JOY) could be worth C$300M+ based on Freeport's own implied valuation at Stage 2 entry. That alone exceeds the current C$209M market cap, before attributing any value to IKE (2020 NI 43-101 resource on file) or DUKE (60:40 JV with Boliden). However, Freeport's earn-in valuation is not a substitute for a formal resource NAV, and pre-resource porphyry stage carries meaningful execution risk. At current stage, the stock appears fairly to moderately undervalued, with substantial upside conditional on the AuRORA resource estimate. Comparable BC porphyry discovery-stage transactions and comparable companies (Amarc trades at roughly 0.3–0.4x the implied JV valuation of JOY alone) support a moderate discount-to-NAV thesis. Score 3: not a screaming deep-value play today, but meaningful resource-stage upside ahead.
analyst_summary:
Amarc Resources (TSXV: AHR) is one of the most credible junior copper-gold explorers in British Columbia, anchored by the Hunter Dickinson group — a team with a genuine 40-year track record of building world-class copper mines in BC. The AuRORA discovery at the 495 km² JOY District is the defining event: a high-grade Au-Cu-Ag porphyry (231 m @ 0.83 g/t Au + 0.22% Cu; multiple other intercepts above 100 m at 0.7–1.0 g/t Au) in the proven Toodoggone-Kemess belt, validated by Freeport-McMoRan's commitment of C$75M in Stage 2 earn-in funding. No Tier 1 miner commits that level of capital without conviction in multi-million-ounce, multi-billion-tonne potential. The February 2026 TWINS and CANYON discoveries extend AuRORA into a multi-deposit district. Robert Dickinson's ~17% personal stake and HDI's institutional backing provide genuine management alignment. The clean share structure (~225M basic) combined with non-dilutive JV funding from Freeport and Boliden is a structural advantage versus peers.

The primary investment risk is that AuRORA has no published NI 43-101 resource estimate — it remains a 'Tier One in the making' story. The current C$209M market cap prices in substantial optionality. Corporate cash is thin (~C$1.56M), though JV funding eliminates the near-term dilution risk for the exploration programs. The 2026 drill season (imminent, Freeport-funded) and the pending AuRORA resource estimate are the specific catalysts to watch. BUY for investors with a 12–24 month horizon who can size appropriately for pre-resource risk.

share_instruments.notes: Dec 2023 PP — $2.77M financing; one-half warrant per NFT unit at $0.08 for 5 years
Stock options granted to management and directors; exact count, strikes, and expiry dates require SEDAR+ financial statement verification
